summary: CC-0082 enables the OpenBao UI in the kind overlay only and documents browser access in the Quick Start. The diff is scoped to four files: one HCL toggle (ui = false → ui = true with a kind-only CC-0082 comment) in deploy/kind/base/kustomization.yaml, a new 'Step 4b — Open the OpenBao UI' section plus an Architecture snapshot row in docs/quick-start.md, and a one-paragraph cross-link in docs/reference/infrastructure/openbao-bootstrap.md back to the new Quick Start step. The production HelmRelease at deploy/flux-system/releases/openbao.yaml is untouched (git diff empty, ui = false preserved, chart version pin unchanged). No code, CI, or test changes.

tests_checklist.item: Tests exist and pass (static: kustomize render + doc review)
Edge cases are covered (N/A — config flip + docs)
Existing E2E tests still pass (REQ-008: infra-stack-health + unseal-openbao.sh)
Live-cluster verification independently reproduced in this review

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing code patterns (headlamp.yaml kind-only comment framing mirrored)
No unrelated changes — scope limited to 4 files declared in the plan
Minimal diff: single HCL line flipped, other listener/storage/service_registration blocks untouched

security_checklist.item: No secrets, credentials, or tokens hardcoded
Self-signed-cert warning does NOT instruct readers to disable TLS verification system-wide or install the internal CA in their system trust store (REQ-004)
Root-token UI flip is kind-only; production posture explicitly preserved in docs and in the production HelmRelease
curl -k usage in smoke checks is scoped to a localhost port-forward session, not a global TLS bypass recommendation

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is as simple as possible (single HCL line + three doc edits)
Kind-only scope enforced: production HelmRelease unmodified (REQ-007)
No new Service, Ingress, listener, or RBAC introduced (non-goals respected)

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No duplicated content: bootstrap reference cross-links rather than restating port-forward/token/warning (REQ-006)

defensive_checklist.item: Self-signed cert expectation set for readers, preventing silent abandonment of the walkthrough (REQ-004)

issues_found.description: Quick Start does not explicitly contrast svc/openbao (client ClusterIP) against svc/openbao-internal (Raft peer-discovery headless Service). The review criteria call this out as MINOR: 'The Quick Start notes that svc/openbao is the client ClusterIP service (not svc/openbao-internal, which is the Raft peer-discovery headless svc).' Readers who run `kubectl get svc -n openbao-system` will see two services and may port-forward the wrong one. The current prose only says the UI is served by 'the same `openbao` Service that backs the bootstrap scripts' without the explicit contrast.
issues_found.fix: Add a short inline hint near the port-forward command, e.g. 'Note: `svc/openbao` is the client ClusterIP Service; `svc/openbao-internal` is the headless Service used for Raft peer discovery and is not the UI endpoint.' — keeps readers on the correct Service in a multi-Service namespace.
issues_found.evidence: Current text at docs/quick-start.md:141-143: 'The OpenBao UI is served by the same `openbao` Service that backs the bootstrap scripts; the kind overlay flips `ui = true` in the standalone Raft config, while the production flux-system overlay keeps it disabled.' — no mention of svc/openbao-internal.

suggested_improvements: Consider adding one line under Step 4b contrasting svc/openbao with svc/openbao-internal so that readers who inspect `kubectl get svc -n openbao-system` do not accidentally port-forward the Raft peer-discovery Service.
next_steps: Optional: address the MINOR-1 svc/openbao vs svc/openbao-internal clarification in docs/quick-start.md Step 4b.

No blocking follow-ups. BLOCKER criteria REQ-002 and REQ-008 depend on a live kind cluster — this sandbox has no docker/cluster access, so those checks rely on the execution records in .planwerk/progress/CC-0082-*.json (Level 2 and Level 4 tasks marked done) and must be reproduced by the PR author in the PR description per task 4.3.
